_doc: IO Profile Runner config. Controls workload size, database tuning, and which profiler kinds are opted in. Any 'profile.*' key absent or false leaves that kind at its default suppression state (most PageCache kinds ship suppressed).

output._doc: Where the .typhon-trace file is written. Relative paths resolve against the process CWD at launch.

database._doc: Tune the page cache smaller than the live set so the workload generates evictions and real async DiskRead/DiskWrite activity. But keep it larger than any single transaction's working set — otherwise reads fill the cache with epoch-protected pages and hit backpressure timeouts. 16 MB = 2048 pages, comfortably holds the read burst's per-tx batches and still forces eviction across passes.

workload._doc: Workload steps. Each stage can be toggled individually; set 'enabled: false' to skip. Stages run in the order listed below.

spawnBurst._comment: Spawns entityCount entities in batches of batchSize. Each batch is one Transaction.Commit, driving WAL writes and dirty-page accumulation.

checkpointAfterSpawn._comment: Forces a checkpoint after the spawn burst. Exercises PageCacheFlush + async DiskWrite + FlushCompleted on a large dirty set in one hit.

spawnCheckpointLoop._comment: Alternating spawn + checkpoint loop — simulates steady-state checkpoint cadence under sustained writes. Each iteration emits its own tick in the viewer (1+1+16+1+1 = 20 visible ticks end-to-end with default stage set).

readBurst._comment: Reads every entity N times. With a small cache most reads fall through to DiskRead, giving per-page tail latency data.

finalCheckpoint._comment: One last checkpoint to drain any remaining dirty pages before trace capture ends.

profile._doc: Per-kind opt-in. 'true' calls TyphonEvent.UnsuppressKind. Any kind NOT listed or 'false' keeps its shipped default (PageCache.* default-suppressed; everything else default-open).
profile._pageCacheKickoff: Kickoff spans — synchronous head of each PageCache operation
profile._pageCacheMarkers: Zero-duration markers emitted inside PageCache operations
profile._pageCacheAsyncCompletions: Completion events emitted from thread-pool continuations — paired with kickoff spans by SpanId, give full async tail latency
profile._engineSpans: Engine-side spans (default-open — listing 'false' will SUPPRESS them for a clean IO-only trace)
